title: aira caryophyllea subsp. cupaniana
intro: Aira caryophyllea subsp. cupaniana, also known as Cupani's Hair-grass or Cupani's Wood-grass, is a plant species within the genus Aira, belonging to the family Poaceae. It is a perennial grass that has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in the Mediterranean region, due to its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and acting as a diuretic, making it a potential remedy for urinary tract infections and kidney stones. Aira caryophyllea subsp. cupaniana can be prepared as an infusion, a decoction, or a tincture to be used in traditional herbal medicine.
